what_it_does: A posted noticeboard note by Tim that comments on another item, agreeing that selection should be hierarchy-aware and framing that behavior along a containment/hierarchy design axis.
observations: It is a note of type 'note' with a commented_on link to board://item-b214f58b.
The body discusses selection at multiple granularities: individual bullet, whole list, intro paragraph, heading, everything under the heading.
It references a recurring 'four axes' vocabulary that Tim mentions.
It states the same principles apply to anything that can be addressed.
There are no code symbols in the file.
standouts: Frames intelligent selection as a containment/hierarchy-axis problem.
Explicitly ties the idea to a recurring 'four axes' design vocabulary.
Uses a commented_on link to reply to another noticeboard item.
conventions: Follows the noticeboard YAML frontmatter schema with id, address, type, source, state, title, author_session, channel, links, created/updated, and history.
Uses a board:// URI for internal addressing and link targets.
Body uses a [paragraph] marker before the prose.
notes: Tim's note endorsing hierarchy-aware selection and situating it within his recurring 'four axes' design framework.
questions: What are the other three axes in Tim's 'four axes' framework?
What is the exact content of the commented-on item-b214f58b?
How would 'intelligent selection' be implemented or represented in the system?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is a paragraph comment on another item; the content matches — it replies to and elaborates on the selection proposal.
intention_signals: To affirm a proposed selection behavior and connect it to a broader design principle, likely to guide future implementation toward the containment/hierarchy axis.
novelty: Treats selection granularity (bullet, list, paragraph, heading, subtree) as a containment/hierarchy problem rather than a flat selection problem.
contribution: Captures a design rationale that links intelligent, multi-level selection to the containment/hierarchy axis and to a recurring four-axes vocabulary, preserving context for selection and interaction design.
summary_for_embedding: A noticeboard note by Tim commenting on intelligent selection, stating that multi-level selection works along the containment/hierarchy axis and that the same principle applies to anything addressable.
intention_for_embedding: To affirm a hierarchy-aware selection proposal and connect it to Tim's recurring 'four axes' design framework.
